skill.name: ai-rag-pipeline
skill.description: Build RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) pipelines with web search and LLMs. Tools: Tavily Search, Exa Search, Exa Answer, Claude, GPT-4, Gemini via OpenRouter. Capabilities: research, fact-checking, grounded responses, knowledge retrieval. Use for: AI agents, research assistants, fact-checkers, knowledge bases. Triggers: rag, retrieval augmented generation, grounded ai, search and answer, research agent, fact checking, knowledge retrieval, ai research, search + llm, web grounded, perplexity alternative, ai with sources, citation, research pipeline
skill.summary: Build RAG pipelines that combine web search with LLMs for accurate, sourced responses
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill uses standard bash command substitution syntax to invoke the legitimate inference.sh CLI tool for web search and LLM queries. Network requests go to authorized services (inference.sh, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter). The pipe-to-shell pattern at line 16 is a standard CLI installation method documented to only download and verify binaries.

high_findings.title: External Commands Detected
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 66 instances of external command execution using bash command substitution $(...). These are legitimate CLI tool invocations to call the infsh CLI for search and LLM operations. No user input is passed unsanitized to these commands.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Bash $(...) syntax is standard for command chaining, not a security vulnerability. Commands invoke fixed CLI tools with structured inputs.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests Detected
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 9 hardcoded URLs. These point to legitimate services: inference.sh (the skill platform), example.com (placeholder URLs in examples), and documentation links.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests are core functionality for a research tool - web search and LLM API calls are expected and legitimate.

low_findings.title: Installation Script Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 16 uses 'curl | sh' pattern for CLI installation. This is a standard installation method used by Docker, Node.js, and many other major projects. The skill documentation notes the installer only downloads and verifies SHA-256 checksums.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard installation pattern with documented security measures (checksum verification).

content.user_title: Build RAG Pipelines with Web Search and LLMs
content.value_statement: AI agents and researchers often produce responses without current information or sources. This skill enables RAG pipelines that combine real-time web search with LLMs to generate accurate, sourced, and verifiable responses.
actual_capabilities: Execute web searches using Tavily and Exa search engines
Extract content from specific URLs for analysis
Generate grounded responses using Claude, GPT-4, or Gemini via OpenRouter
Build multi-step research pipelines with source aggregation
Create fact-checking workflows with evidence verification
Produce research reports with proper citations
limitations: Requires API keys for Tavily, Exa, and OpenRouter services
Search results depend on search engine quality and indexing
Does not store or index documents locally - operates on query-time retrieval

use_cases.title: Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Automate research by searching multiple sources, aggregating findings, and generating comprehensive reports with citations.

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking Tool
use_cases.description: Verify claims by searching for supporting and contradicting evidence, then generating balanced verdicts with sources.
use_cases.target_user: Journalists, content creators, and verification teams

use_cases.title: AI Agent Knowledge
use_cases.description: Give AI agents the ability to fetch current information and cite sources, making their responses verifiable.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building AI agents and assistants

prompt_templates.title: Simple Search and Answer
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for information on [TOPIC] using tavily search, then provide a summary with sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick research queries requiring current information

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Source Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Run searches on [TOPIC] using both tavily and exa search. Compare the results and identify common themes.

prompt_templates.title: URL Content Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract content from these URLs: [URLS]. Then analyze and summarize the key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep dive into specific web sources

prompt_templates.title: Fact-Checking Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for evidence about this claim: [CLAIM]. Provide a verdict with supporting and contradicting sources.

output_examples.input: Research the latest developments in quantum computing
output: Based on recent searches, here are the key developments in quantum computing:
1. **Error Correction Breakthroughs** - Researchers at Google and IBM have demonstrated significant advances in quantum error correction...

output_examples.input: Fact-check: AI will replace 50% of jobs by 2030
output: - McKinsey report suggests 30% of current tasks could be automated...
- World Economic Forum projects AI will create 97 million new jobs...
CONCLUSION: The claim is overstated. AI will transform work rather than simply replace jobs.

best_practices: Use specific, contextual queries rather than vague terms to improve search relevance
Summarize long search results before passing to LLMs to manage context length
Always request source citations in LLM responses for verifiability
anti_patterns: Using overly broad queries like 'AI news' instead of specific topics
Passing raw search results directly to LLMs without summarization for long outputs
Skipping source attribution - always verify AI responses cite their sources

faq.question: What API keys do I need for this skill?
faq.answer: You need API keys for the inference.sh CLI, which provides access to Tavily, Exa, and OpenRouter services. Sign up at inference.sh to get started.

faq.question: Can I use my own OpenAI or Anthropic API key?
faq.answer: This skill uses OpenRouter as the LLM gateway, which provides access to Claude, GPT-4, and Gemini. Direct API key integration would require skill modification.

faq.question: How does this compare to Perplexity?
faq.answer: This skill provides similar functionality - web search + LLM for sourced answers. You control the pipeline and can customize it for specific use cases.

faq.question: Can I search internal documents?
faq.answer: No, this skill searches the web via Tavily and Exa. For internal document search, you would need a separate RAG solution with local indexing.

faq.question: Is the search traffic unlimited?
faq.answer: Search usage depends on your API plan with the respective providers. Check inference.sh pricing for limits.

faq.question: Can I customize which LLM is used?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes examples for Claude Sonnet, Claude Haiku, GPT-4o, and Gemini 2.5 Pro. You can modify the app ID in the examples.
